desc:
There is no way to tell if this path has been used recently. The presence of
fresh growth would indicate that the trail was old and forgotten. However, the
lack of moisture makes the sprouting of anything almost impossible. The surface
of the ground is rock hard, making it very difficult to track anything.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
The ground is littered with dead bark and bleached wood. The small, scraggly
trees are so dry that their branches snap in the slightest wind. Dropping a
torch or a hot coal here would certainly have dire consequences, as you and the
surrounding area would quickly be engulfed in flames.
name: A Stunted Forest

desc:
In the heyday of the Kingdom of the North, this road was widely used, as it was
the main artery of traffic between the prosperous Dunedain settlements of Arnor.
Now nothing remains to reminisce of its former glory. The road lies in
disrepair, beaten and worn by the feet of the occasional traveller and
adventurer.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Draughts of warmer wind blow in your face from a northern direction. Looking to
the source of the reassuring wind you discover an uncomfortable change, the once
golden prairies you fondly recall being here have been scorched and blackened.
It looks as though a mighty battle has just been waged. The road continues
East-West.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Whether started by you or someone else, this fire is out of control. You cannot
see farther than in front of your face. Your eyes sting from the billowing smoke
and it is so hot it burns!
name: A Raging Brush Fire

desc:
The area south of the road still contains remnants of wildlife, but to the
north, the land grows continually paler. Black hills, scraped of all vegetation
stand out bare against the sky, wisps of smoke and steam hang in the heavy air.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Eastwards, thick smoky clouds obscure your sight. To the south, a small trodden
path branches off this road. Both north and south offer little more than minor
vegetation, small brush and weeds, atypical of the plains surrounding you. The
only signs of life at all in fact, would be men who once toiled here to make
this road.
name: Old East Road

desc:
A feeling of dread enters into your heart and dampens your soul. You want
nothing more than to leave this foul area behind you. You can no longer bare to
look around at the bleak landscape. You grit your teeth and stare straight
ahead.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Black smoke and ash swirls around you, restricting your breathing and making you
gag. Even the bleak landscape, no sight for sore eyes, has been blotted out by
this thick foulness. You wipe the soot from your eyes and try to simply focus on
the road ahead of you.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The need for concentration is evident here. Dark swirling clouds of dirt and
debris diminish your view and lash at your clothing. The road vanishes ahead
into swirling clouds of evil winds, tread carefully and do not stray from the
road.
name: Old East Road

desc:
This path seems to have been carved by wild animals rather then by any civilized
race. Winding back and forth across the coarse grass and small shrubs, it heads
south and east, to an unknown destination.
name: Country Path

desc:
Overlooking the Old East Road, this ridge lies a couple of miles south of the
road. The ground slopes steeply down towards the road creating a steep cliff. At
the bottom of the cliff lie rocks from countless rockslides and from this angle
you can see that a pile of rocks to the east cover an opening in the cliff.
name: Along the Ridge

desc:
The cliff wall that ends this ridge abruptly is full of cracks as it bends
slightly outwards in the west. To the south-east, along the plains, the
Mitheithel flows southward until it joins with the Bruinen and becomes the
Greyflood.
name: Along the Ridge

desc:
To the north a sheer cliff has formed before the rocky plains below. On the
northern edge of the plain an old road winds towards an ancient stone bridge in
the north-east. To the south-west, as far as the eye can see, the highlands of
central Eriador spread out. To the east and south a precipice drops into the
river far below.
name: Along the Ridge

desc:
A sheer cliff that is impossible to climb looms above you to the north and west.
The cliff is full of cracks which small birds have used when building their
nests. As it does in many other places, the river once again bends and now it
continues south.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
The Mitheithel twists and turns. The water flows swiftly between a pair of huge
boulders, and anything swept along by the river risks being smashed against
them. The riverbanks are quite steep and littered with stones.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
On the floor of this cave lies the skeleton of some large biped creature. It
probably got trapped here when some stones fell on top of the cave mouth to the
east. The walls of this cave are cracked and there are some old and now almost
completely faded paintings on them.
name: Shallow Cave

desc:
A high cliff wall blocks the way to the west and south. The wall is several
hundred feet high and grey, except for those places where bushes grow in the
cracks. Close to the north is the old east road. The road is not in a good shape
and it is quite hard to see the difference between it and the surrounding rocky
plains.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
The Old East Road to the north-east and a high ridge to the south surround these
bleak and barren fields. No animal life exists here but excrements and tracks
from small creatures can be found on the ground. The ground is quite rocky and
the only vegetation here are some scant patches of heather.
name: Fields beside the Road

desc:
These rocky plains lie nested between the Old East Road to the north and a high
ridge line to the south that will transform into a grey hill to the east. Some
heather grows between the rocks on the ground where a snake or two will
sometimes hide.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
The river Mitheithel surrounds these hills on the southern and eastern side.
North of the hills a steep and rocky slope leads down to a bridge that crosses
the river in the north-east. Many of the bushes stand close to the river and
between them the coarse grass grows tall.
name: Hills

desc:
The river bends once again and flows south from here. Further downstream the
river twist and turn causing much erosion to the riverbanks. On the rocks
closest to the water green alga and moss grow uninhibited forming a slippery
surface. Not many traces of animal life can be found around here but some bird
droppings do appear on the rocks.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
The ravine cut by the river is more narrow and steep here. At the bottom of the
ravine there are small ledges close to the water which contain coarse grass and
stunted bushes. On the northern ledge lies a dead tree, which was uprooted
somewhere upstream, as well as some loosely scattered gravel which has been
washed away from the nearby road.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
The gravel laden Old East Road curves to the north in order to avoid a rocky
slope to the east. At the southern edge of the road patches of heather slowly
give way to the rocks that lie scattered across the plains. The skyline in the
far north is dotted by the blunt mountain peaks.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
Huge stone blocks lie close to the northern edge of the Old East Road and beyond
the boulders the blunt peaks of some distant mountains can be seen against the
horizon. The Old East Road has been poorly maintained, for large gaps and holes
in the structure have formed making travel by wagon impossible. The only
vegetation that can be seen from here are scant patches of heather mixed in with
some weeds.
name: Old East Road

desc:
In the cracks of the road surface tough weeds grow, but apart from those little
vegetation can be seen. On the south side of the road there is a large cliff
rising sharply out of the rocky plains.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
The Old East Road bends here, avoiding the huge boulders to the east. While you
can move east, the boulders and rocks will make travel in that direction
difficult. Along the side of the road there are some scant patches of heather
and sharp rocks scattered about. The wildlife here is almost non-existent, with
only small birds making an occasional venture.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
This stretch of the Old East Road is straight and narrow. It passes between two
ridges that block the view to the north and south. The road is in poor condition
due to lack of proper maintenance for many decades. On the side of the road some
bushes grow, and scattered among the bushes various rocks can be found.
name: Old East Road

desc:
A dark somber veil seems to pervade this area. The grassy fields and mountains
fade from view and all that can be seen is the smoking embers of a torched and
barren land. You search your memory for some news of a battle, waged near here,
but recall no such event. What could have possibly have happened?
name: Old East Road

desc:
Here the Old East Road bends to the south as it bypasses several huge stone
blocks which prevents easy passage the west. The heather, a few yards from the
roadside, bows slightly in the wind that blows from the surrounding plains. The
road slowly dips to the east as it traverses the lower lands besides the river
Mitheithel.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
The sounds of a river swirling against its bank can be heard to the east, the
same direction the road slopes steeply towards. On the southern side of the road
the terrain turns rocky where several large boulders can be seen. There is an
impressive bridge, made out of stone, a little ways towards the south- east and
next to the bridge there are some partially standing buildings.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Part of the road near the river seems to have been washed away in a flooding,
while other parts are slippery and undermined. The riverbank next to the road is
steep and muddy as the road turns south towards more stable ground. The Last
Bridge, which can be seen on the horizon, appears quite massive even from this
distance. It is made out of stone, with three great arches that have withstood
the ravages of time.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
The Last Bridge can be seen to the east, at the bottom of a short steep slope.
It was built by the men who once dwelt here, ages ago, but no longer remain. The
Old East Road bends northward to bypass the steep and rocky slope to the west.
Far away to the east, beyond the Mitheithel, another river lies. It is
Loudwater, the Bruinen of Rivendell. The road runs along the edge of the hills
for many miles from the bridge to the Ford of Bruinen which lies before the
Loudwater.
name: Bend in the Old East Road

desc:
A big and sturdy structure, this is the last bridge, before Tharbad, crossing
the river Mitheithel which flows from its sources in the Ettenmoors. It was
built by the men of Rhudaur before they fell under the shadow of Angmar. The
bridge is made of rectangular stone blocks, but some of them are missing and
others have crumbled under the weight of time. From below comes the sound of
water swirling against the bridge's three great arches. On the eastern side of
the river, the decaying remains of a small cluster of buildings can be seen.
name: The Last Bridge

desc:
The western bank of the river is steep and littered with rocks from the Old East
Road while the eastern bank is more benign and easier to ascend. Just south of
here is the Last Bridge. From here, as the river flows under its three great
arches, the bridge looks very large. Time has taken its toll on the bridge as
some of its large stone blocks have crumbled and others have been washed away.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
The differences between the east and west riverbanks are startling. On the
western bank very little vegetation grows while on the eastern bank a grove of
trees, starting from the edge of the river, grows. A bit north the Mitheithel
merges with one of its tributaries causing the river here to be wider than
further upstream.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
Here the Mitheithel, flowing down north and east from the Ettenmoors, merges
with one of its tributaries which originated in the harsh highlands to the
north-west. North of the river lies an ancient fortress, protected on three
sides by the water.
name: At a Confluence

desc:
This ridge continues both to the east and west. A couple of miles to the east it
curves northward where it eventually ends at the base of some barren hills. The
view is spectacular up here on the ridge and down below, to the south, lies the
Old East Road. Twisting and turning its way between Amon Sul to the west, and
the river Bruinen to the east, the road stretches far into the distance.
name: On a Ridge

desc:
Here, heather covers much of the ground, except for those patches which are
broken up by thickets and large boulders. To the south a ridge rises up and
above it the shape of another, higher ridge, can be discerned against the sky.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
The most common form of vegetation growing on these rocky plains is heather,
which is broken up into small patches by large rocks and an occasional bush. A
ridge gently rises out of the surrounding plains in the distant south and con-
tinues to the south-east where barren hills eventually take over.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The plains stretch to the west as far as one can see and to the north until they
reach a distant river. The vegetation here consists mostly of heather and brush,
some of which appears to have been grazed upon. But no tracks remain to identify
the species of the grazer.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The rocky plains to the west gradually transform into these barren hills. Not
much grows here, just some grey heather and a few stunted bushes, but further to
the north lies lush vegetation and a forest. At the foot of the forest a
tributary of the river Mitheithel flows gently down from the highlands until it
finally joins the river in the east.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
North from here one of the river Mitheithel's tributaries flow towards the
south-east. On the other side of the tributary, to the far north, lies the
ruined town of Cameth Brin which was once the capital of the Rhudaur kingdom.
Now almost nothing remains of that town as it was destroyed in one of the
countless wars that plagued this region. The hills stretch far from here in all
directions and they seem almost devoid of life.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
The hills stretch as far as can be seen to the south and west but to the north
they end abruptly bounded by one of the tributaries to the river Mitheithel. The
hills that are visible are bleak and barren and the only indication of wild life
is an occasional bird searching for food.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
The remains of old wagon tracks lead east towards a ford in the river. Among the
tracks some weeds and coarse, bleak grass have begun to grow. To the west the
hills begin to rise gently and continue for several miles.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
A dark and ruined fortress lies on the east side of this tributary which feeds
the river Mitheithel. While not much of the fortress is left, one of the towers
has managed to remain standing. Here the river is rather shallow, thus there
appears to be no problem crossing to either side.
name: Tributary of the Mitheithel

desc:
The ground slopes steeply down towards the river which is just east of here.
There, the river Mitheithel joins with one of its tributaries and together they
rush to the sea in the distant south. The ground is littered with many small,
sharp rocks between which grow a few stunted bushes.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Steep hills surround you on all sides. Growing upon the slopes of the hills are
a few patches of heather and coarse weeds but for the most part the ground is
barren and grey. To the south the hills gradually recede and eventually give way
to a flat plain.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
The only way to avoid travelling through these steep hills is to use the Old
East Road, some 3 miles to the south. The hillsides all about are littered with
sharp rocks. While these rocks are all grey in colour, they can be found in a
variety of different shapes and sizes. Between the rocks patches of heather and
a few small blueberry bushes grow. Apart from those plants, little vegetation
can be seen.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
A ridge, that formed to the far west, has transformed into these rolling hills.
While not much vegetation grows here, a few weeds and some small patches of
heather manage to thrive. Tracks from some kind of small animals criss-cross the
ground. They seem rather old and worn making it hard to determine what kind of
animal made them.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
The ridge that began far off to the west ends here. To the east, hills extend
outwards for many miles. South of the ridge, down a short but steep slope, the
grey plains are cut off by a road.
name: Ridge

desc:
A sheer-faced ridge lies just north of here, where it cuts across the land to
the west, before turning northward. The ground all around you is strewn with
rocks, and the only vegetation which survives is comprised of several types of
weed. Not far to the south a road snakes off into the distance, leading
eventually to Rivendell, to the far east.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
Sharp rocks and stones litter the ground all about. At the edge of the road,
just to the south of here, the rocks appear to be somewhat regular and
rectangular, perhaps ancient cobblestones which once paved the way. Small stands
of stunted trees can be seen here and there, but these are few and far between.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
To the south the Old East Road twists and turns between rocky grounds and steep
ridges. The ground is rocky but strands of stunted trees can be seen nearby and
weeds abound in the cracks on the surface of the road. The rocky terrain of this
area appears to be ideal for snakes but prey for these creatures might be scarce
since wildlife is sparse in this area.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
The soil here is dry and cracked even though a river flows close by to the east.
At the southern edge of the plains lies the Old East Road, an ageing
thoroughfare, which looks to be in dire need of repair. Heather and weeds fill
innumerable cracks in the surface of the road, while rocks and stones lie
scattered about.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
The old wagon tracks along the river, just east of here, lead from the south
where the Old East Road can be found. The riverbank is steep and is likely to be
difficult to ascend. In the north-west hills rise gently out from the
surrounding plains and to the north-east the blunt shapes of a couple of towers
can be seen against the horizon. The vegetation here is scarce, consisting
mostly of weeds and a few patches of heather.
name: Plains beside the Road

desc:
Huge boulders lie scattered across the terrain making travel here difficult.
Between the boulders, dense thickets have grown covering the treacherous ground.
The Old East Road lies to the south, avoiding this inhospitable landscape.
Towards the north and south the plains slowly transform into grey hills, that
look barren from here.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
This is the gatehouse of what used to be a mighty fortress. Small slits in the
western wall allowed a small number of archers to control the entrance to the
fortress. The gatehouse was built from large rectangular stone blocks and it has
withstood years of erosion, though damage during the war weakened the far
northern wall to an extent that it eventually collapsed under its own weight
destroying the stairs that once led to the second floor. Of the enormous gates
that once guarded the eastern entrance only the bronze hinges remain, but to the
west a very rusty portcullis still stands. To the south is a structure that
looks like it used to be the guardroom.
name: Ruined Gatehouse

desc:
Once this was a place where the gate guards spent time between watches. Someone
has pushed the rubble, formed from rotten furniture, into a corner and placed
bedrolls against the eastern wall. A crooked table made from several rough
planks stands in the middle of the room and the remnants of a simple meal can be
found upon it.
name: Guard Room

desc:
Slits, several feet apart, for archers have been cut along the wall. These slits
and a rectangular hole in the ceiling are the only ways for sunlight to enter
this room. The stone stairwell continues up towards the next level, but some
steps are missing along with a large portion of the northern wall.
name: The Tower's Second Floor

desc:
Just below, some of the stair's steps are missing along with a large portion of
the wall. The stairs spirals upwards towards the third level of the tower and
slits for the archers are cut into the wall at regular intervals. Bits of
useless rubble, upon many of the stairs, remains where it fell many years ago.
name: Crumbling Stairs

desc:
While this tower was once several stories high, now this floor, the third, is
the highest floor remaining. All that remains of the floors above this one is
the huge pile of stone just beyond the southern wall. During clear days, a keen
eye can see for many miles from here. The partial roof above is what used to be
the fourth floor and is made out of planks supported by huge wooden beams some
of which are missing and those remaining extremely rotten. To the north and to
the east very narrow doorways lead to old and crumbling battlements.
name: Top of the Ruined Tower

desc:
Built to guard the southwestern corner of the Rhudaur's Kingdom, this strong-
hold was a pivotal spot, for any enemy army approaching from the west across the
last bridge was easily repulsed as long as this fort stood. From here a small
bastion of soldiers could watch and harass any enemy approaching from the Last
Bridge. However, when the Kingdom of Rhudaur fell, the fortress became a refuge,
but eventually it was overrun and left in ruins. This battlement, the western
section, has a few holes but is mostly intact. However, the eastern wing of the
battlement, which took substantially more damage, lays in ruins and that which
remains is unsafe.
name: Southern Battlements

desc:
This battlement leads from the southwestern tower to the roof of a massive
gatehouse. Once, a crenellated parapet lined the top of the wall granting
archers excellent cover, but now it has crumbled along with portions of the
gangway's railing leaving many small sticks and stones upon the boards which
form the floor resulting in unstable footing.
name: Western Battlements

desc:
Here, high above a ford in the river Mitheithel, an effective defence could be
made of the fortress gates. From here, archers could fire upon anything
approaching the gates with the only dead-ground being an area just below the
walls. To the north of here, many years ago, the battlements were reduced to
ruins during an assault upon the fortress.
name: Above the Gatehouse

desc:
The size of the gatehouse below you, with its thick walls, must have caused it
to be a daunting prospect for conquering. During sieges this section of the
roof, a few yards back from the wall, was used to store spare arrows and other
supplies needed for defence. A narrow hatch in the roof leads to the floors
below.
name: The Gatehouse's Roof

desc:
Scattered across the floor are the remaining parts of the mechanism that was
once used to raise and lower the portcullis below. The eastern wall, which faces
the courtyard, has a few narrow vertical slits that enabled archers to defend
the gatehouse after a breach of the main gates. Against the northern wall a set
of stairs leads upwards towards the roof.
name: The Gatehouse's Armoury

desc:
The gatehouse was considered to be the defence's linchpin in the event of an
attack upon the fortress. Thus, the gatehouse was designed to accommodate
numerous archers protected behind thick stone walls. To enhance the fortress's
defence and to make an assault upon it even more costly, three large holes were
cut into the floor to allow the defenders to pour hot oil upon those assaulting
the portcullis. Other than a huge hole in the northern wall, which was made
during a successful conquest of the fortress, the only other openings are the
archers slits in the western wall. Being one of the few remaining rooms that is
still able to provide shelter in the fortress, this room has often been used by
wanderers as a refuge since the fall of the fortress.
name: The Second Floor

desc:
The river curves around this steep bank to the north and west. On the bank lies
some old trees that were uprooted upstream by some long forgotten storm and
swept along by the river until they struck this bank. To the south-east the old
farmland continues for many miles. A couple of miles to the north a stone bridge
crosses the river. The bridge looks huge, even from this distance, and its three
great arches span the Mitheithel with ease.
name: Riverbank

desc:
Just north of here is a large and impressive stone bridge known as the Last
Bridge. For the bridge to have survived these many years, the men who built it,
ages past, must have been master stone masons. The eastern shore of the river
has eroded away forming a steep bank that is impossible to climb. The river
makes a sharp turn here and, for many miles, flows westward.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
Below this slope and to the east the road is sweeping around the hills and
finally connects to a large stone bridge that spans the river. The ground is
strewn with sharp rocks and the passage down the slope, which leads to the
bridge, is impassable. South of here the ground slopes more gently into the
river. At the river bank only traces of the sparse vegetation can be found.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
A bit north of here a large stone bridge spans the river Mitheithel. Next to the
bridge on the east side, nested among some trees, the ruins of several stone
buildings remain. South of here, a long time ago, crops were once grown and
harvested but now the only thing that grows over there are some weeds and a few
lonely strands of wheat.
name: Riverbank

desc:
A dark sombre veil seems to pervade this area. The grassy fields and mountains
fade from view and all that can be seen is the smoking embers of a torched and
barren land. You search your memory for some news of a battle, waged near here,
but recall no such event. What could have possibly have happened here?
name: Old East Road

desc:
The skyline is flat and unending along this stretch of the road. Westwards
however, a violent storm whips dust swirls high into the air. Here however, the
air is remarkably still, allowing the unpleasant odor of rotting flesh to
permeate the dusty air.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Black ash spills over the land and road, surrounding you in an unholy darkness.
The plains around you exhibit no signs of life, just blackened stumps of bush
and limp weeds. The road continues south and west here, but travels deeper into
the blackness westwards.
name: Old East Road

desc:
The road runs straight west and east into swirling clouds of evil winds. The
dark swirling clouds are made of dirt and debris that diminish your view and
lash at your clothing. Tread carefully and do not stray from the road.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Evil winds stir dirt and loose weeds from these lifeless plains, obscuring your
view and making the road difficult to follow. Surrounding you, black clouds of
cruel design blow foul breath onto the scarred land. Leaving the road would be
dangerous.
name: Old East Road

desc:
Dirt rains down from the air upon you like a heavy spring shower. Accumulating
in your pockets and folds, you can even feel its added weight upon your body.
Squinting to shield your eyes, you try to scan for the road, but to no avail.
Caught in this constant blowing wind, you lose all sense of orientation, only
the thin strands of weeds blowing fiercely in the wind give you a sense of
direction.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
The ground rises here, bringing you slowly out of the fierce winds preying on
the lower lands. Once perhaps this was a grassy slope, covered with bushes and
weeds. Now, broken wood and bits of metal zip through the air, threatening to
pierce or bash you without warning. The ground, broken rocks and dirt, add dust
to the air shrouding you in a fog of particles.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
The wind quiets here, ending its howling reign at the cusp of this rise. Below
you and to the north, the brown grass waves in the wind, but it is of a more
natural persuasion. The forced winds around you ebb back, gathering strength
near the fissure, their source of power.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
Wizard spells are of course powerful, it is easy to remember seeing mages
throwing herbs into the air as they sang words of spells, summoning clouds and
rains upon once shining bright skies. Perhaps that is what the foul minions of
Morgoth have done. Plagued this land with eternal winds of evil. Or perhaps this
dying land suffers from nothing more than the fatal lick of Melkor's powers,
songs of discord ruining Eru's vision, regardless, the land around you lies dead
and miserable with no end in sight.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
Broken stumps and bits of wood are all that hint of this lands former life here.
Once perhaps a green forest, now they stand as monuments and relics to a life
long lost. Surely the winds could not have caused all this death? Rumors of the
strength of Angmar trigger your thoughts. Perhaps this was engineered by the
foul WitchKing, for the air around you blows with malevolence.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
A few patches of hearty vegitation struggle here to take root. Choked in dirt
and debris, they lay bruised and pale, clinging to life. The majority of the
area is nothing but broken dirt and rock, a true wasteland. Checking to find
some sort of reference or orientation, your view is lost in the constant winds
around you.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
The earth lies barren here. After years of fighting with constant winds, the
land lies plucked clean, like a goose without a feather. All that remains is
loose dirt and broken debris. Gnarled pieces of wood, tattered bits of clothing
lay tossed apathetically, the victims of a powerful and hateful wind.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
The ground here rolls ever so slightly, once no doubt a waving lush plain of
grass and flowers. Now, like a broken and battered enemy, all that remains is
dust and rock. A few weeds dig into the lifeless land, the last remaining
warriors of a beaten and dying land.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
Seemingly lost within this fog of dirt and particles, you look vainly for the
reassuring road. Winds swirl around you, lifting your body with incredible
force. A few more gusts like that, and you could find yourself flying through
the air! Glancing around for some sort of reference, your gaze is broken by the
endless view of brown.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
Tiny whirlwinds sprout around you, swirling dust into plumes and sending them
spinning to the sky. The fiery glow of Arien or Stars is blotted by the blanket
of dirt constant falling downwards upon you. Shaking the dirt from your body
like a heavy snow, you scan for a way out of this wasteland.
name: Windswept Ground

desc:
Like a crooked dagger into the heart of Ea, here an open fissure travels deep
underground, perhaps even into the Eternal Flame. Waves of incredible heat belch
from this opened wound, mixing into the cool air and creating turbelent winds.
Dirt and debris kick up around you, obscuring your view and threatening to
topple you without warning.
name: Cracked Ground

desc:
This small passageway through the hills was created to ship materials easily to
and from the under-construction mine in this vicinity. It's crudely crafted,
with no support save the rock above it. It now appears to be abandoned.
name: Secret Tunnel

desc:
Crunch.. Crunch.. Crunch.. the sound of feet on the dry vegetation is the only
audible sound you hear. Scanning for signs of life, the occasional snake or
vulture are your only companions, and certainly not friendly ones. Opening into
a vast plain of broken, seething, ashen ground, these hills gently roll for
miles and miles, until your sight can perceive no more.
name: Bleak Hills

desc:
The sound of crunching feet on the dry vegitation is the only audible sound you
hear. Scanning for signs of life, the occasional snake or vulture are your only
companions, and certainly not friendly ones. Opening into a vast plain of
broken, seething, ashend ground, these hills roll onwards for many miles, until
your sight can perceive no more.
name: Bleak Hills

desc:
Leaving the bitter winds behind, you feel no safer here. The burnt ground, as
though touched by the fierce Anar, lies scarred and black. The charred bushes
leave a bitter taste in your throat and mouth. Your body, covered in dried sweat
and filth, begs for rest and sleep. Yet here, in this opened barren land visible
for miles, rest would not be prudent.
name: Bleak Hills

desc:
Rising before you is a blackened slope, further to the west you see more of the
same dreary, blackened ground. It is as if war were waged here from which the
land never healed. Looking southward you glimpse part of the Old East Road,
wishing desperately you could get back to it.
name: Charred Slope

desc:
The ground, brown and parched lies gasping for life around you. Huge vicious
nettles seem to be the only plant thriving here, pricking your skin and bringing
blood to their sharp pointy spear-like petals. Searching for a sense of
direction, you seem small and insignificant in the bleak area of the Lone-lands
around you.
name: Stinging Nettles

desc:
You stumble out of those windy barren plains, thankful to have escaped with your
life. Quickly scanning the area, it doesnt look much more pleasant. Surrounding
your sight is dry, cracked, burnt grass and towering stinging nettles. Your body
involuntarily shudders as the stinging nettles bite into your mottled and
sweating flesh.
name: Stinging Nettles

desc:
A few hardy trees remain here, the last guardians of a once lush forest. But
weathered after years of evilness, they now stand bent towards the ground,
leaning on one another for support. The entire effect is haunting, you look up
at a network of interwoven trees, blocking the heavens above.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
The pond before you appears relatively dried out and lifeless. Small pockets of
water have collected in some of the deeper depressions of this uneven pond bed
making drink possible. The water however, is dark and gritty.
name: Small Pond

desc:
You peer closely at this mishapen hill trying to discover its secrets. Then it
dawns on you, this is in fact not a hill at all, but a man made structure,
conquered by the wild elements of nature. A small pock marked archway stands
over the rubbled entrance, inscribed upon it the words: "TarnGurth", or rough
westron translation, Gates of Death.
name: The Strange Hill

desc:
From this vantage point you can see an outlay of the area. Westwardly, the ashen
land continues, occasional vents of steam can be seen whistling out from under
rocks. To the east, you are puzzled. It looks as though you see a brown grassy
hill, but its odd shape makes you curious. Far to the south, the Old East Road
is visible.
name: Charred Slope

desc:
This small cut in the rock appears to have once held all the mining equipment
used in excavation. Scattered about the floor lie broken lanterns, bits of metal
and splintered ore carts. Along the wall, dirty but strong rope hangs from
wooden pegs, a true essential for any miner.
name: Storage Room

desc:
Up ahead of you the sky peers through the domed cavern walls, cool air mixes
with the hot dry heat from farther within creating a constant yet gentle breeze.
From the far wall, rain water and moisture collects and leaks down a crevice in
the ceiling, collecting in a small cool pool of water. The entire affect
refreshes your sweat stained body.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
Your heart, so recently free and light, drops like a stone pit.. Yes, it is true
you are standing outside among the sky and air, but towering walls surround you
on all sides. The opening, is in fact, the bottom of a huge blackened sinkhole.
There appears to be no access to the surface from here.
name: An Opening

desc:
You appear to be travelling along the main passageway of the mine. In between
the wooden planked walls the wet earth sweats with warm water. The wood itself
appears warped and compromised, its integrity lost after years of moisture and
stress.
name: Main Passageway

desc:
The corridor here begins to descend further into the earth, the air takes on a
musty mildew-like smell. Off to your right a wooden door prevents further access
leaving you only further downwards into the dark, as your option.
name: Main Corridor

desc:
Here marks the entrance to the caverns of TarnGurth. The walls surrounding you
perspire with underground warmed water, giving the entrance a humid misty
atmosphere. Parts of the muddy walls have been smoothed away and arched with
wooden supports, yet most of the cavern has been left in excavation form, with
the walls gashed and gutted, the strikes of pickaxes still visible. It seems
obvious that this mine was long abandoned before it was ever finished.
name: Inside the Rubble

desc:
This part of the tunnel has partially caved in, but you notice a section of the
roof still standing. Beneath it, the bones of foxes and rabbits lay cluttered on
the floor. Along the farside of the wall, a large crack in the floor sends
intense waves of heat lofting into the air. This looks like a nice warm spot for
some animal to live.
name: Among the Rubble

desc:
Is your imagination playing tricks on you or did you just feel the cool lick of
a soft breeze upon your sweat stained forehead? You cautiously advance forward,
the sound of trickling water echos from the walls ahead.
name: Eastward Branch

desc:
Enough light is shining from ahead that you are finally able to see the walls
again. To your surprise, the dirt sodden earth has been replaced by smooth worn
rock, small veins of copper and gold streak through the wall, magnifying the
light source, and sending it sparkling down the length of the tunnel.
name: Eastward Branch

desc:
Warm currents of heat circulate in the air around you, yet deep inside you feel
cold and afraid. It occurs to you, maybe this mine wasn't abandoned after all,
maybe the workers left their merry homes fully expecting to return, and vanished
in the depths of these mines, never returning... What would put such a thought
in your mind?
name: Main Corridor

desc:
Fierce heat lashes at your face from farther ahead. The walls weep here with
hot, scalding water. It appears as though this was to have been a side corridor,
but digging was probably stopped when they realized they were dangerously close
to an underground steam spring.
name: Side Shaft

desc:
AAAAAIIIIIYYYYEEEEEEEEEEE!!! Your body shudders in sharp unyielding pain as the
hot steam from below sears the flesh from your body. Your eyes explode seconds
later, sparing you the sight of a mist of red plume travelling up the vent....
the last remains of your mortal body.
name: Scalding Steam

desc:
The rocks blast you with stored and active heat, nearby there must be a very hot
flow of molten rock or steam. The tunnel twists and curves here making it
difficult here to see what is ahead. A small door to the west is also
noticeable.
name: Westward Passage

desc:
Here the tunnel breaks into two seperate directions, westards or eastwards. Both
choices plunge you further down into the dark and intolerable heat. You cant
help but have an eerie feeling about this place. Perhaps you should just turn
back?
name: Fork in Tunnel

desc:
A thick darkness hangs in the air, barely penetrable by your light. The sea of
black around you makes you grasp for the moist earthen walls in reassurance.
Only seconds later, a powerful wave of nausea sweeps over you, your nostrils are
assailed with the smell of rotting flesh and stale blood.
name: The Darkened Corridor

desc:
Turning the corner you are stunned and horrified by the sight that greets you.
This large open cavern, that was probably a principle area of mineral
extraction, has been turned into a decaying uncovered gravepit. The numerous
small chutes that normally serve to probe for mineral content, have been stuffed
full with broken limbs, decaying bodies, and tattered remains. The effect is
quite sublime, the once open tubes now resemble a gruesomely improvised
catacomb. A weathered board stands here, displaying a warning...
name: Opened Cavern

desc:
Stepping down into the cellar you are shocked by the warm water surrounding your
ankles... this area is flooded! Barrels that once probably held wine and drink
now float empty along the debris strewn water. Upon the shelves, bits of moldy
bread still remain untouched.
name: Cellar Storage

desc:
A large oaken desk dominates this room, covered about it still lay the schematic
drawings and blueprints for this mine. Above the chair a tattered and musty
banner once displaying some proud crest, now is worm eaten and
undistinguishable. The room looks alltogether undisturbed, yet you still feel a
presence nearby.
name: Abandoned Office

desc:
You stumble on the bones and corpses under foot. All around you the pale white
faces of the dead stare blankly at you, some viciously mauled, others you would
almost mistake for alive. You walk quietly, fearful that one loud noise might
wake them out of their daze. A pale corpse stares directly at you.....
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
The ground here is sticky to walk on, your feet making sucking noises with each
step you take. A sense of danger washes over your body, you wish your feet would
stop making so much noise! Cautiously glancing around you, you notice fine
threads of silk swaying gently, only thing is, there is no wind.
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
Large rocks here lay covered with a thin glaze. Long sinewy threads of web
spindle to the ceiling creating a huge tent in the centre of the room. Nestled
underneath this moist shelter, HUGE spider eggs, easily the size of boulders,
lay pressed up against the rocken walls. If these are the husks of infant
spiders, you shudder to think of the adults....
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
Thick rope sized strands of web cut your path, hanging from the walls and and
corridors around you. Navigating through these webs, and avoiding entanglement
is becoming increasingly difficult. All around you, shadows spring and disappear
from view. Perhaps it is time to turn around.
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
That last step didn't seem too wise. Encircling you, thick ropey glue like webs
greedily grab at your clothing. You try desperately to weave a path through the
dense webs, but any second you could become totally trapped.
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
Attempting to navigate through this dense wall of web is proving very difficult.
Tough thick web snare your clothing, forcing you deeper into their grasp. Can
you escape? You lunge forward with every ounce of strength, I guess you shall
soon find out.
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
Up ahead you feel the familiar warmth and humidity associated with this entire
cavern.. only, it somehow "feels" different. The hot current comes in regular
timed pattern, followed by a faint, guttural noise. Whatever is ahead of you, it
sounds large.
name: Inside the Catacombs

desc:
Large spindles of webs drop from the high vaulted ceiling, travelling downwards
into a large widening arc. Before you lies a macabre scene, mummified remains of
Elves, Dwarves and other unlucky pests hang lifeless within the webs. You catch
a glint of metal here, a bundle of something there, the whole area reminds you
of a bunch of puppets, suspended by strings.
name: A Natural Cavern

desc:
The sweat rolls off your face in torrents, stinging your eyes and drenching your
body. You wonder how anyone could have worked in these conditions. Up ahead
however the darkness breaks, some bright light source further ahead is
reflecting back off the shining rocks. It affords you minimal light.
name: Eastward Passage

desc:
Whether started by you or someone else, this fire is out of control. You cannot
see farther than in front of your face. Your eyes sting from the billowing smoke
and it is so hot it burns!
name: A Raging Brush Fire
